mcqs.question: Which of the following best defines a distributed system?

options.text: A collection of independent computers that appears as a single coherent system
A system with multiple processors sharing the same memory
A network of computers using the same operating system
A single computer with multiple processing units

mcqs.explanation: A distributed system is defined as a collection of independent computers that appears to its users as a single coherent system, with components communicating through message passing.

mcqs.question: Which type of transparency hides the fact that a resource may be moved to a different location while in use?

options.text: Location transparency
Migration transparency
Relocation transparency
Replication transparency

mcqs.explanation: Relocation transparency hides the fact that a resource may be moved to a different location while in use, such as during process migration for load balancing.

mcqs.question: In the RPC architecture, what is the primary function of the client stub?

options.text: To execute the remote procedure on the client side
To marshal parameters and communicate with the server stub
To manage network connections between client and server
To authenticate the client to the server

mcqs.explanation: The client stub is responsible for marshaling parameters into a message format and communicating with the server stub to make the remote procedure call appear local to the client.

mcqs.question: Which challenge in distributed systems refers to the ability to handle increasing numbers of users and resources?

options.text: Heterogeneity
Concurrency
Openness
Scalability

mcqs.explanation: Scalability is the challenge of designing systems that can handle growth in users and resources without significant performance degradation.

mcqs.question: What is the key difference between RPC and RMI?

options.text: RPC uses TCP while RMI uses UDP
RPC is for procedural programming while RMI is for object-oriented programming
RPC is faster than RMI
RPC can only be implemented in C while RMI is for Java

mcqs.explanation: The fundamental difference is that RPC follows a procedural paradigm (calling remote procedures) while RMI follows an object-oriented paradigm (invoking methods on remote objects).
